qualifiedClaim.summary: ARC ships a cryptographically signed, fail-closed, intent-aware governance control plane with shared executor registry resolution, signed route-selection evidence, multi-hop receipt lineage, and a shared lifecycle contract across the qualified authoritative HTTP, MCP, OpenAI, A2A, and ACP surfaces.
constraints: Only the supported authoritative protocol surfaces are claim-eligible.
Compatibility helpers remain non-authoritative and non-claim-eligible.
Lifecycle semantics may be adapted through the shared deferred-task contract where the underlying protocol does not expose identical transport behavior.
This gate proves the technical control-plane thesis; it does not prove ecosystem-wide market dominance.

nonQualifiedClaims: ecosystem-wide universal partner adoption beyond the qualified authoritative surfaces and explicit operator/runbook proof

gateConditions.summary: Claim-eligible authoritative bindings never silently default to Native; route selection is explicit or registry-derived with signed route evidence.
CrossProtocolOrchestrator resolves target executors through one shared registry contract rather than edge-local branch tables.
At least two authoritative multi-hop routes execute across more than one protocol boundary with continuous receipt lineage and route evidence.
Governed intent is a first-class input to route planning rather than a passive metadata echo.
The control plane can select, attenuate, or deny candidate routes based on policy, capability, intent, and runtime availability.
Signed route-selection evidence is surfaced consistently on qualified HTTP/API, MCP, OpenAI, A2A, and ACP authoritative paths.
Stream, cancel, resume, and partial-output semantics are defined by one shared lifecycle contract used by every claim-eligible protocol surface.
Bridge fidelity and publication gating are derived from runtime route and lifecycle capability evidence rather than static schema hints alone.
Compatibility-only helpers remain isolated, non-default, and explicitly non-claim-eligible.
The qualification package combines exercised authoritative route tests with explicit operator-boundary evidence instead of relying on a single in-process narrative.
Operator-grade deployment and reviewer material document trust boundaries, route planning behavior, failure handling, and receipt continuity for the qualified surfaces.
The stronger original technical control-plane claim upgrades only after FABRIC3, CTRL3, LIFE3, and ECO3 evidence is complete.
The docs separate technical control-plane qualification from any still-unproved comptroller-of-the-agent-economy market-position thesis.
